description_long: This object is a robust, rectangular jersey barrier made primarily of concrete, featuring a slightly sloped central face and reinforced with vertical end supports. The surface is weathered, showing patches of rust and discoloration, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The top edge is flat and broad, while the sides taper inward toward the base, providing stability and resistance to vehicle impact. Metal rods or reinforcements are visible at the ends, likely for structural integrity and ease of handling. The overall finish is rough and matte, with visible signs of minor scuffs and surface wear. This type of barrier is commonly used to separate lanes, protect pedestrians, or block vehicle access in urban environments.
description: A heavy, weathered concrete jersey barrier with sloped sides, reinforced ends, and a rough, matte finish showing minor scuffs and rust patches.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the narrow, sloped profile and the reinforced vertical end supports.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the broad, flat top, sloped central face, and visible rust patches along the surface.
description_view_2: Opposite end-on view, mirroring the narrow, sloped profile and end supports seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the flat top, sloped face, and weathered surface with minor scuffs.
